title: Webhook Configuration in CloudDash
content:
CloudDash webhooks deliver real-time HTTP notifications when events occur in your organization, enabling custom integrations with internal tools, ticketing systems, and automation workflows.

Supported Events: alert.triggered, alert.resolved, alert.acknowledged, integration.status_changed, dashboard.created, dashboard.updated, team.member_added, team.member_removed, and billing.payment_failed. Enterprise plans add audit.event_logged and sla.breached events.

Creating a Webhook: Go to Settings > API & Integrations > Webhooks > Create Webhook. Provide the target URL (must be HTTPS with valid TLS certificate), select events to subscribe to, and optionally configure a custom secret for payload signature verification.

Payload Format: Webhooks deliver JSON POST requests with Content-Type application/json. Each payload includes: event (string), timestamp (ISO 8601), organization_id, data (event-specific object), and webhook_id. Example alert.triggered payload includes alert_id, rule_name, severity, metric_value, threshold, and affected_resources array.

Signature Verification: CloudDash signs payloads using HMAC-SHA256 with your webhook secret. The signature is sent in the X-CloudDash-Signature header as sha256=HASH. Verify by computing HMAC-SHA256 of the raw request body with your secret and comparing to the header value. Reject requests with mismatched signatures.

Retry Policy: Failed deliveries (HTTP non-2xx or timeout after 10 seconds) are retried with exponential backoff: 1 minute, 5 minutes, 30 minutes, 2 hours, and 24 hours. After 5 failed attempts, the webhook is disabled and Admins receive an email notification. Re-enable from the Webhooks settings page after fixing the endpoint.

Testing: Use the Send Test Event button to deliver a sample payload to your endpoint. CloudDash logs the last 100 delivery attempts per webhook with status code, response time, and error details under Webhook Delivery Log.

IP Allowlisting: Webhook requests originate from CloudDash IP ranges documented at docs.clouddash.io/webhook-ips. Allowlist these ranges if your endpoint is behind a firewall.
